title: appendicula cristata
intro: Appendicula cristata, also known as the 'crested appendicula', is a type of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
